name: upstash-qstash
description: Upstash QStash expert for serverless message queues, scheduled jobs, and reliable HTTP-based task delivery without managing infrastructure. Use when: qstash, upstash queue, serverless cron, scheduled http, message queue serverless.
capabilities: Publish messages to HTTP endpoints with guaranteed delivery
Schedule recurring cron jobs without servers
Implement webhook delivery systems with retry logic
Configure message deduplication for critical operations
Set up delay scheduling for time-based task execution
Create URL groups for batch message delivery
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 9 potential security issues, all are false positives. Line 69 contains markdown code formatting (backticks around skill names), not shell execution. Lines 3, 59, and 72 contain the word 'critical' in documentation text, not weak cryptographic algorithms. This is a pure documentation file with no executable code. Safe to publish.
valueStatement: Implement reliable serverless messaging without managing infrastructure. Schedule HTTP-based tasks, cron jobs, and webhook delivery systems using Upstash QStash.
userTitle: Build Serverless Message Queues and Scheduled Tasks
limitations: Cannot reach private or localhost endpoints in production
Requires publicly accessible HTTP endpoints for callbacks
Rate limits apply based on QStash plan tier
Callback endpoints must respond quickly to avoid timeouts

useCases.title: Schedule Periodic Database Maintenance
useCases.description: Set up recurring cron jobs to trigger database cleanup, backup operations, or data synchronization tasks at fixed intervals without managing servers.

useCases.title: Build Reliable Webhook Delivery
useCases.description: Implement webhook delivery systems that automatically retry failed deliveries and verify signatures to ensure messages reach their destination.

useCases.title: Delay Heavy Processing Tasks
useCases.description: Schedule resource-intensive operations like video processing or report generation to run later during off-peak hours using delay scheduling.

promptTemplates.title: Send a Simple Message
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the upstash-qstash skill to send a message to my endpoint https://api.example.com/tasks with the payload {"task": "process-order", "orderId": 12345}
promptTemplates.scenario: You need to trigger an HTTP endpoint asynchronously

promptTemplates.title: Schedule a Cron Job
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me set up a QStash cron job that calls https://myapp.com/cron/cleanup every day at 2 AM UTC using the upstash-qstash skill
promptTemplates.scenario: You want to run recurring tasks on a schedule

promptTemplates.title: Configure Webhook Delivery
promptTemplates.prompt: Using upstash-qstash, show me how to set up a message to https://webhook.site/xxx with signature verification and retry configuration
promptTemplates.scenario: You need to ensure reliable webhook delivery with security

promptTemplates.title: Implement Delayed Processing
promptTemplates.prompt: Use upstash-qstash to schedule a message to be delivered to https://api.example.com/process in 30 minutes with deduplication enabled
promptTemplates.scenario: You want to defer a task to run later with deduplication

bestPractices: Always verify QStash webhook signatures using both your signing keys to authenticate incoming requests
Design callback endpoints to respond quickly (within 30 seconds) to avoid timeouts and enable proper acknowledgment
Use deduplication for critical operations to prevent duplicate processing when messages are retried
antiPatterns: Do not skip signature verification on callback endpoints, as this allows anyone to send fake messages to your system
Avoid sending large payloads directly in messages; instead send references or IDs to keep messages lightweight
Do not expect QStash to reach localhost or private network endpoints in production environments

faq.question: What is Upstash QStash?
faq.answer: QStash is a serverless message queue and scheduling service that delivers HTTP requests to your endpoints reliably. You send messages to QStash, and it handles delivery, retries, and scheduling.

faq.question: How do I verify webhook signatures?
faq.answer: Extract the signature from the Upstash-Signature header, verify it using your QStash signing keys, and reject requests with invalid signatures. Always use both the current and next signing key during rotation.

faq.question: Can I send messages to localhost endpoints?
faq.answer: No, QStash cannot reach localhost or private network endpoints in production. Your callback endpoints must be publicly accessible. Use tools like ngrok for local development testing.

faq.question: What happens if my endpoint is down?
faq.answer: QStash automatically retries failed deliveries with exponential backoff. You can configure custom retry behavior and set up failure callbacks to handle undeliverable messages.

faq.question: How does message deduplication work?
faq.answer: When you enable deduplication with a deduplication key, QStash ensures only one message with that key is processed. This prevents duplicate execution of critical operations during retries.

faq.question: What are the rate limits?
faq.answer: Rate limits depend on your QStash plan tier. Check your plan documentation for specific limits on messages per day and requests per second. Implement backoff handling in your code.
